meta.name: Cinnamon Raisin Bread
meta.subtitle: Soft, lightly sweet loaf • swirled cinnamon sugar • breakfast/toast baseline • fresh milled or commercial flour

source.notes: Enriched sandwich-style loaf with cinnamon swirl and raisins. Optimized for toast and French toast. Includes fresh-milled guidance and 70/30 blend option.

yield.notes: One standard 9x5-inch loaf; yields ~12–14 slices depending on thickness.

tasteProfile.notes: Warm cinnamon aroma, sweet raisins, and a defined swirl—excellent toasted with butter.

ingredients.label: Flour (bread flour preferred; or all-purpose; or fresh milled wheat flour—see Fresh-Milled Notes)
ingredients.notes: Bread flour gives better rise and slice structure; AP is softer and still works.

ingredients.label: Instant yeast (or active dry yeast)
ingredients.notes: About 2 1/4 teaspoons. If using active dry, bloom in warm milk (see steps).

ingredients.label: Sugar (for dough)
ingredients.notes: About 1/4 cup. Keeps crumb soft and lightly sweet.

ingredients.label: Raisins
ingredients.notes: About 3/4 cup. Dark or golden raisins both work.

ingredients.label: Warm water (for soaking raisins, optional but recommended)
ingredients.notes: Helps raisins stay plump and prevents them from stealing moisture from the crumb.

ingredients.label: Cinnamon-sugar filling (mix before use)
ingredients.notes: See next ingredients for exact weights.

ingredients.label: Flour (for filling; helps reduce swirl separation)
Unsalted butter, melted (optional, for swirl adhesion)
ingredients.notes: About 1 tablespoon. Brush onto dough before adding filling.

ingredients.label: Egg wash (optional: egg + splash of milk)

steps.title: Optional: soak raisins (recommended)
instructions: Cover raisins with warm water for 10 minutes to plump.
Drain well and pat dry with a towel so they don’t add extra water to the dough.

steps.title: Optional: bloom active dry yeast (if using)
instructions: If using active dry yeast, stir yeast and a pinch of sugar into the warm milk (use from recipe milk).
Rest 5–10 minutes until foamy. If using instant yeast, skip blooming.

steps.title: Mix dough
instructions: In a large bowl (or mixer bowl), combine flour, sugar, salt, and (if using instant) yeast.
Add warm milk, egg, softened butter, and vanilla (if using).
Mix until a shaggy dough forms and no dry flour remains.

steps.title: Knead until smooth; then add raisins
instructions: Knead until dough is smooth and elastic: stand mixer 6–8 minutes; by hand 10–12 minutes.
Add raisins and knead briefly just until evenly distributed (1–2 minutes).
Dough should be soft and slightly tacky; avoid over-flouring.

steps.title: First rise (bulk)
instructions: Place dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and rise until doubled, 60–90 minutes.

steps.title: Prepare pan and filling
instructions: Grease a 9x5 loaf pan (or line with parchment sling).
Mix brown sugar, cinnamon, and 10g flour for the filling.
Optional: melt 14g butter for brushing onto the dough before filling (helps swirl stick).

steps.title: Shape loaf with cinnamon swirl
instructions: Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface and gently press into a rectangle.
Roll out to about 8x16 inches (20x40 cm), with the short side roughly matching the pan width.
Optional: brush the rectangle lightly with melted butter for adhesion.
Sprinkle cinnamon-sugar evenly, leaving a 1-inch border on one long edge (so the seam can seal).
Starting from the long edge opposite the clean border, roll up tightly into a log.
Pinch seam closed; pinch ends and tuck under. Place seam-side down in the pan.

steps.title: Final proof
instructions: Cover and proof until the loaf crowns 1–1.5 inches above the rim, about 45–75 minutes.
Loaf should spring back slowly when gently pressed.

steps.title: Bake
instructions: Heat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Optional: brush top with egg wash for shinier browning.
Bake 35–45 minutes until deep golden and internal temp is 190–200°F (88–93°C).
If top browns too fast, tent loosely with foil for the last 10–15 minutes.

steps.title: Cool and slice
instructions: Cool in pan 10 minutes, then remove and cool on a rack at least 45 minutes before slicing (swirl sets as it cools).
Optional: brush with melted butter after baking for a soft, glossy crust.

freshMilledNotes.summary: Fresh milled flour brings great flavor but can reduce loft. Blend or adjust hydration for a softer sandwich-style crumb.
guidelines: Best soft-loaf option: 70% fresh milled flour + 30% bread flour (same total flour weight).
If using 100% fresh milled (unsifted), consider sifting out some bran for better rise and swirl integrity.
Fresh milled may need +10–25g milk depending on wheat and fineness; dough should be soft and tacky.
Add a 10-minute rest after initial mixing (before kneading) to hydrate bran for smoother dough.

variations.name: 70/30 whole wheat option (heartier but still soft)
variations.change: Use 70% whole wheat (or fresh milled) and 30% bread flour within the 420g flour total. Add +10–20g milk if needed.
variations.notes: More wheat flavor and nutrition; still sliceable.

variations.name: Cinnamon swirl without raisins
variations.change: Omit raisins. Keep filling the same.
variations.notes: Great for kids who don’t like raisins.

variations.name: Cranberry-orange loaf
variations.change: Replace raisins with dried cranberries; add 4g orange zest to dough; optional 10g orange juice in place of milk (reduce milk slightly).

variations.name: Extra-defined swirl (reduced separation)
variations.change: Use the 10g flour in filling (already included) and brush dough lightly with melted butter before filling; roll tightly and seal seam well.
variations.notes: Helps swirl adhere and reduces big gaps.

storage.freezer: Slice and freeze up to 2 months; toast from frozen.
storage.reheat: Best toasted. For whole loaf warming: wrap in foil and warm at 300°F (150°C) for 10–15 minutes.

notes: For the best toast: slice once fully cooled; toast until edges crisp and cinnamon is fragrant.
If your swirl separates (big tunnels): roll tighter, seal seam firmly, and include flour in the filling (already specified).
Raisins can burn on the surface—keep them mostly inside the dough when shaping.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions.
